description: Document the normalized target invariant where templates are authoring-only and instantiated contracts are the sole live billing source
Inventory every runtime use of `template_contract_id`, `contracts.is_template`, and `contract_lines.is_template` and record the cutover scope in the plan scratchpad
Add a strict pre-cutover verification pass that proves canonical `contract_template*` records exist for every legacy template artifact still referenced by the system
Define whether `client_contracts.template_contract_id` remains as provenance-only metadata or is fully removed in the normalized end state
Remove billing-engine contract-line resolution fallback from `template_contract_id` to template-backed contract IDs
Remove billing-engine discount resolution fallback that treats `template_contract_id` and `contract_id` as interchangeable live keys
Guarantee that template-to-contract instantiation clones all runtime-billing data needed so live billing never reads through templates after assignment
Convert `shared/billingClients/templateClone.ts` semantics so template provenance is one-way copy metadata rather than an active runtime lookup dependency
Split contract endpoints from template endpoints so contract reads never silently return template-backed resources
Remove template-to-contract DTO adapters that flatten templates into `IContract`-shaped responses in billing actions
Refactor contract detail/list loaders so template and contract lookups are explicit and never share a fallback-by-ID path
Split template line repositories/actions from contract line repositories/actions at the server/package boundary
Remove mixed-mode repository helpers that accept a single ID space for 'maybe template, maybe contract' line operations
Refactor template update/delete flows so they only touch `contract_template*` tables and never mutate instantiated contract storage
Audit and remove contract/template compatibility branches in billing dashboard loaders and detail-switcher UI flows
Audit and remove compatibility branches in client contract actions/models that backfill or depend on `template_contract_id` as live runtime state
Audit and remove compatibility branches in renewal flows that preserve hybrid template/contract runtime behavior
Audit and remove compatibility branches in API services that treat templates as contract fallbacks or backfill template IDs from contract IDs
Rewrite or retire `server/scripts/verify-template-migration.ts` so it validates the normalized model instead of duplicated legacy/separated storage
Rewrite or retire `server/scripts/contract-template-decoupling.ts` so it no longer preserves hybrid template fallback behavior
Define and implement the cleanup migration sequence for removing obsolete legacy template columns and runtime assumptions
Add a gated cleanup migration that removes live reliance on `contracts.is_template` after runtime and API cutover are validated
Add a gated cleanup migration that removes live reliance on `contract_lines.is_template` after template-line separation is validated
If selected by the final design, add a migration to remove or null out `client_contracts.template_contract_id` once it is provenance-only or obsolete
Retire or quarantine legacy template rows in `contracts` once canonical template tables are proven complete and unused by runtime flows
Retire or quarantine legacy template rows in `contract_lines` once canonical template line tables are proven complete and unused by runtime flows
Document operator runbooks for preflight, cutover, rollback posture, and post-cutover validation in the plan scratchpad
Update billing architecture docs to describe the post-normalization split between template authoring and live contract runtime data
Update types/interfaces to separate template DTOs from instantiated contract DTOs where shared contract-shaped compatibility currently exists
Ensure downstream integrations and exports continue to operate from instantiated invoice/contract data only after template fallback removal
Add DB-backed validation gates proving no runtime code path requires legacy template rows before destructive cleanup lands
Add DB-backed post-cutover validation proving templates are stored canonically while contracts remain fully billable without template-side fallback
Document template instantiation as the only supported transfer boundary from authoring tables into runtime contract data
Guarantee that editing a template after contract instantiation cannot alter live contract runtime behavior without an explicit future reapply workflow
Guarantee that deleting a template after instantiation only affects authoring-side assets and provenance, never live runtime contract behavior
